document.title: Session B Complete Word Data Export — JSON Specification
document.status: Draft — for CC verification against database schema and live export production
document.change_note: v1.1: Corrected against actual schema (database-table-analysis.md updated 2026-04-15). Field name corrections throughout. Superseded fields retained as consistency check targets. wa_meaning_sense and mti_term_cross_refs added. wa_verse_term_links excluded (engine internal). wa_session_b_dimensions retained as consistency check target. word_registry.inference_note constraint flagged. Legacy fields noted. All tables included — Session B audits data preparation completeness, not just analytical content.
document.purpose: Defines the exact structure, sections, fields, and source tables for the complete word data export that Session B v5.0 loads as its authoritative input. Every table and field in the database that pertains to a registry is included. Session B uses this export to: (1) verify that all data preparation processes have functioned correctly, (2) identify gaps and inconsistencies, (3) take corrective action where data preparation has failed. Superseded fields are included because Session B must verify consistency between superseded and replacement fields. Legacy fields are noted but not audited for content.
document.session_b_audit_principle: Every field in this export is present for a reason. Session B does not skip fields because they appear sparse, legacy, or superseded. It asks: is this field correctly populated? Is it consistent with related fields? If not — why not, and what corrective action is required?

meta.description: Export identity and version information. Claude AI confirms version at Step 1.1 before any audit work begins. Per GR-DB-001 — never assume currency.

fields.export_version: INTEGER — auto-incremented per day. Must be confirmed against CC at session start.
fields.schema_version: TEXT — database schema version at export time
fields.instruction_version: TEXT — CC instruction version that produced this export

registry.description: All fields from word_registry for this registry. Includes legacy and sparse fields — Session B assesses whether each is correctly populated or correctly NULL.

audit_notes.inference_note: CONSTRAINT: Session B patches must NEVER overwrite this field. It is researcher-only. Read for context only.
audit_notes.legacy_fields: phase1_input_file, phase1_output_file, phase2_datasets, category_hint are legacy. Include in export. Session B notes if they contain data conflicting with active fields but does not audit for content.
audit_notes.dim_review_status: Populated for Dimension Review completed registries only. Session B checks consistency with wa_dimension_index confidence levels.
audit_notes.sb_classification: NULL until Stage 2 Pass 4. If populated, Session B verifies it matches the analytical picture from prior work.

fields.source_list: TEXT — High Confidence / Low Confidence / Inferred / Missing Inner Being Words / Programme Addition
fields.category_hint: TEXT — LEGACY. Sparse (17%). Superseded by cluster_assignment. Include — note if conflicts.
fields.phase1_input_file: TEXT — LEGACY. Sparse (22%). Include — content not audited.
fields.phase1_status: TEXT — Pending / Complete / Excluded / In Progress
fields.phase1_output_file: TEXT — LEGACY. Sparse (15%). Include — content not audited.
fields.phase2_datasets: REAL — LEGACY. Sparse (14%). Include — content not audited.
fields.phase1_term_count: INTEGER — Phase 1 snapshot only. Not updated by Session B.
fields.dimensions: TEXT — comma-delimited dimension labels from Dimension Review
fields.inference_note: TEXT — RESEARCHER ONLY. Never overwrite. Read for context.
fields.sb_classification: TEXT — confirmed_characteristic / plausible / uncertain / instrumental / relational_only
fields.dim_review_status: TEXT — Dimension Review completion stamp. Sparse (16%).
fields.dim_review_version: TEXT — instruction version used for Dimension Review. Sparse (16%).

file_index.description: wa_file_index rows for this registry. One row per Session A JSON file. The parent for all WA term and verse data. Session B verifies that file index entries are present and correctly linked.

audit_notes.part_number: Only populated for multi-part files. Session B checks that split registries have all parts present.
audit_notes.schema_version: Sparse (28%) — pre-versioning imports lack this. Accept NULL for older entries.

fields_per_file.registry_id: TEXT — string form of registry number
fields_per_file.schema_version: TEXT — Sparse (28%). NULL for older imports.
fields_per_file.category: TEXT — LEGACY. Sparse (6%). Superseded by cluster_assignment.

terms.description: All terms for this registry from wa_term_inventory joined to mti_terms. Active terms (delete_flagged=0, mti_terms.status IN ('extracted','extracted_thin')) are the analytical set. Deleted terms are included for Stage 1 audit — Session B verifies that deletions are justified and that phase2 flags on deleted terms are correctly handled. Per GR-DATA-001.

audit_notes.god_as_subject: SUPERSEDED by mti_term_flags. Do not write. Session B checks consistency: if god_as_subject=1 here, a GOD_AS_SUBJECT flag must exist in mti_term_flags. Inconsistency is a Stage 1 finding requiring corrective action.
audit_notes.somatic_link: SUPERSEDED by mti_term_flags. Do not write. Same consistency check as god_as_subject.
audit_notes.meaning_meaning_numbered: SUPERSEDED by wa_meaning_parsed. Include — Session B checks whether wa_meaning_parsed is populated for this term. If wa_meaning_parsed is NULL but meaning is present here, that is a data preparation gap.
audit_notes.status_note: NULL across nearly all records. No pipeline purpose. Include — Session B notes if unexpectedly populated.
audit_notes.term_owner_type: 457 NULL from pre-OWNER/XREF era. Session B flags any NULL term_owner_type as a data preparation gap requiring CC resolution.
audit_notes.evidential_status: NULL until Stage 2. Sparse (2%) — only set for Session B completed registries.
audit_notes.lsj_entry: Greek terms only. Sparse (29% of total — 100% of Greek terms). Accept NULL for Hebrew terms.

fields_per_term.strongs_number: TEXT — H/G prefix. 9 NULL from legacy — flag as gap.
fields_per_term.occurrence_count_qualifier: TEXT — Sparse (9%). NULL when not applicable.
fields_per_term.meaning: TEXT — SUPERSEDED by wa_meaning_parsed. Include for consistency check.
fields_per_term.meaning_numbered: TEXT — SUPERSEDED by wa_meaning_parsed. Include for consistency check.
fields_per_term.also_spelled: TEXT — Sparse (0.2%). NULL when not applicable.
fields_per_term.god_as_subject: INTEGER — SUPERSEDED. Include for consistency check against mti_term_flags.
fields_per_term.somatic_link: INTEGER — SUPERSEDED. Include for consistency check against mti_term_flags.
fields_per_term.status_note: TEXT — NULL across nearly all records. Include — note if unexpectedly populated.
fields_per_term.retention_note: TEXT — NULL unless term retained despite low evidence
fields_per_term.term_owner_type: TEXT — OWNER or XREF. NULL = pre-era gap, flag for CC.
fields_per_term.owning_registry_no: INTEGER — for XREF terms: the owning registry

quality_flags.description: Engine-derived DATA_COVERAGE flags from wa_data_quality_flags. Reset and re-derived on every audit_word run. Session B checks these are present and reflect current data state.

phase2_flags.description: Analytical flags from wa_term_phase2_flags. Advisory — prior-session hypotheses. Session B verifies each against verse evidence in Step 1.4. Source and raised_date sparse (29% NULL) for flags pre-dating M14.

mti_flags.description: Analytical flags from mti_term_flags. Session B verified flags — authoritative for GOD_AS_SUBJECT and SOMATIC properties. Session B checks consistency with wa_term_inventory.god_as_subject and somatic_link.

related_words.description: Lexical relatives from wa_term_related_words. Re-inserted on STALE_TERM updates. Session B checks that related words are present for terms with expected lexical families.

mti_cross_refs.description: Cross-references between MTI terms from mti_term_cross_refs. Used for root family and related term identification. Session B checks whether expected cross-references are present, particularly for root family completeness (gap G-13).

meaning_parse.description: Structured meaning parse from wa_meaning_parsed (engine step A7). Session B verifies A7 ran correctly — parsed_meaning_id should be populated and wa_meaning_parsed should have a row. If meaning is in the legacy field but wa_meaning_parsed is NULL, that is a data preparation gap.

wa_meaning_sense.description: Sense-level parse from wa_meaning_sense (15,981 rows). Each row is one sense of the term. Session B uses senses as the primary meaning input for Pass 1. Verifies that senses are present for terms where A7 ran.

wa_meaning_stem.description: Stem-level parse from wa_meaning_stem (13 rows). Hebrew stem-structured terms only. Very sparse — Session B notes if absent for expected Hebrew stems.

root_family.description: Root family data from wa_term_root_family. 22% gap from pre-backfill era (gap G-13). Session B checks whether a root family record exists. If absent for a term that should have one, raises a CC directive to create the root record before entity links can be written.

lsj_parse.description: LSJ lexicon parse from wa_lsj_parsed (2 rows only — prototyped, not scaled). Greek terms. Session B notes if present but does not audit for content — pipeline not operationalised.

deleted_terms.description: Terms with delete_flagged=1 OR mti_terms.status='delete'. Included for Stage 1 audit. Session B critically evaluates each deletion — is it justified? Are phase2 flags on deleted terms correctly handled (irrelevant if term is deleted)?

fields_per_term.phase2_flags: array — phase2 flags on this deleted term. Session B assesses each as irrelevant and notes in observations log.

verse_records_summary.description: Summary verse statistics per term from wa_verse_records. Session B uses this to verify verse corpus completeness — not to read individual verses (verse text comes through verse_context anchor verses). Session B checks: does the verse count match the occurrence count reasonably? Are there zero-verse OWNER terms? Note: wa_verse_term_links is engine-internal (span matching) and is NOT included in this export.

fields_per_term.total_verse_records: INTEGER — count of wa_verse_records where delete_flagged=0
fields_per_term.delete_flagged_count: INTEGER — count of deleted verse records
fields_per_term.book_coverage: TEXT — list of distinct book names covered

verse_context_groups.description: All verse context groups for OWNER terms from verse_context_group, joined to wa_dimension_index for dimension assignments. This is the primary analytical input for Session B Stage 2. Session B verifies: all OWNER terms have groups, all groups have descriptions, all groups have dimension assignments at CLAUDE_AI or RESEARCHER confidence, all groups have dominant_subject populated.

audit_notes.set_aside_reason: Extreme sparseness (0.8%) is expected — only populated from VCB-032 onward. Do not flag as a gap for batches prior to VCB-032.
audit_notes.vertical_pass_flag: All 0 currently — not yet activated. Include — Session B notes if unexpectedly populated.
audit_notes.dominant_subject: Sparse (27%) programme-wide — reflects Dimension Review progress. For this registry, must be populated on all groups before Stage 2 begins.

fields_per_group.notes: TEXT — Sparse (9%). NULL when not needed.

dimension_assignment.description: From wa_dimension_index (1:1 with verse_context_group). Session B verifies: dimension present, confidence at CLAUDE_AI or RESEARCHER, dominant_subject populated. AUTOMATED confidence is a Stage 1 trigger for Dimension Review sub-process.

fields.dimension: TEXT — emergent dimension label. NULL (7%) = not yet reviewed.
fields.dominant_subject: TEXT — GOD / HUMAN / OTHER_HUMAN / UNSEEN / NONE. NULL = not yet assigned.
fields.notes: TEXT — Sparse (40%). Analytical notes where present.

anchor_verses.description: Anchor verses for this group — is_anchor=1 in verse_context, joined to wa_verse_records. These are the primary reading material for Stage 2.

related_verses.description: Related (supporting) verses for this group — is_related=1. Included for completeness but not primary reading material.

set_aside_verses.description: All set-aside verses (is_relevant=0) for this registry's OWNER terms. Session B may challenge set-aside decisions during Stage 1 audit. set_aside_reason is extremely sparse (0.8%) for batches prior to VCB-032 — absence of this field does not constitute a gap for those batches.

fields_per_verse.set_aside_reason: TEXT — extremely sparse for pre-VCB-032 records. NULL is expected.

session_b_findings.description: All rows from wa_session_b_findings for this registry — active (delete_flag=0) and deleted/superseded (delete_flag=1). Per GR-OBS-005 — deleted records are retained for audit. Session B reviews all prior-phase findings and dispositions each one during the relevant Stage 2 pass.

fields_per_finding.file_id: INTEGER FK — NULL for registry-level findings
fields_per_finding.pass_ref: TEXT — NULL for pre-v5.0 findings. Session B notes if absent.
fields_per_finding.delete_flag: INTEGER — 0 active / 1 superseded or set aside

finding_entity_links.description: All rows from wa_finding_entity_links for findings in this registry. New table created 2026-04-15. Will be empty for registries where Session B v5.0 has not yet run. Session B uses this to check whether prior findings have entity links, and writes new entity links at each pass close.

fields_per_link.entity_type: TEXT — term / verse / group / dimension / registry / root_family / cross_registry

session_b_dimensions.description: Rows from wa_session_b_dimensions for this registry. 2 rows programme-wide — largely superseded by wa_dimension_index. Included because Session B must verify consistency: if a row exists here, its dimension flags must be consistent with the wa_dimension_index assignments for this registry. If inconsistent, Session B raises a finding. If consistent and redundant, Session B notes it as a candidate for retirement via RESEARCHER_DECISION when sufficient evidence accumulates.

session_research_flags.description: All wa_session_research_flags rows for this registry. Separated by session_target for Stage 1 clarity. Note: flag_code is stored as a string directly on the row — no FK constraint exists. The category field is now available via wa_quality_flag_types lookup.

session_b_flags.description: Flags requiring resolution within this Session B run — session_target='B', resolved=0. Hard gate: all must be resolved before Stage 2 closes.

fields_per_flag.file_id: INTEGER FK — Sparse (38%). NULL for registry-level flags.
fields_per_flag.strongs_reference: TEXT — Sparse (49%). NULL for registry-level flags.

sd_pointers.description: Session D pointers previously raised for this registry — session_target='D'. Included for Pass 6 review and Analytical Brief Section 8.

dimension_review_log.description: wa_dim_review_cluster_log row for this registry's cluster (if present). Session B uses this to confirm that Dimension Review for this cluster was formally completed and which instruction version was used.

cross_registry_links.description: Session A era cross-registry links from wa_cross_registry_links for this registry. Historical reference. connecting_term stores transliterations, not Strong's numbers — cannot be joined programmatically. Session B includes these as context for Pass 6 connection verification.

fields_per_link.linked_registry_id: INTEGER FK — NULL for 7 unresolved links
fields_per_link.connecting_term: TEXT — transliteration form. Cannot be joined to mti_terms.

correlation_signals.description: Correlation signals for this registry — primary input for Pass 6. CC to confirm: are correlation signals embedded in the export or provided as a separate file? If separate: confirm whether Pass 6 requires the correlation file to be attached at session start or only when Pass 6 begins.
correlation_signals.status: TBC — CC to confirm source table name, field names, and whether embedded or separate file

patch_history.description: Recent patch and engine run history for this registry from engine_run_log and word_run_state. Session B uses this to understand what has been done to this registry and when. Provides Stage 1 audit context.

statistics.description: Derived counts for quick audit reference at Step 1.1. Session B uses these to spot obvious gaps before reading the full data.

fields.null_owner_type_count: INTEGER — terms with term_owner_type NULL. Should be 0.
fields.groups_without_dimension: INTEGER — dimension NULL in wa_dimension_index. Should be 0 before Stage 2.
fields.terms_without_mti_cross_refs: INTEGER — 0 mti_term_cross_refs rows. Note if unexpected.
fields.thin_evidence_findings: INTEGER — thin_evidence=1. Must be 0 at session close.
fields.session_b_flags_unresolved: INTEGER — session_target=B, resolved=0. Must be 0 at Stage 2 close.
fields.sd_pointers_raised: INTEGER — all SD_POINTERs for this registry
fields.god_as_subject_mti_flag_count: INTEGER — GOD_AS_SUBJECT in mti_term_flags. Should equal inventory count.
fields.somatic_link_mti_flag_count: INTEGER — SOMATIC_INNER_LINK in mti_term_flags. Should equal inventory count.
fields.session_b_dimensions_rows: INTEGER — rows in wa_session_b_dimensions for this registry. Expected 0 for most registries.
fields.root_family_gap_count: INTEGER — active OWNER terms with no wa_term_root_family row

cc_verification_tasks.description: Tasks for CC before producing the first live export against this spec. CC must confirm each item and return results before the export is considered valid for Session B use.

tasks.question: Does word_export.py currently export all sections in this spec? Specifically: (1) wa_finding_entity_links — new table from 2026-04-15; (2) all 18 fields of wa_session_b_findings including the 9 new lifecycle fields; (3) wa_quality_flag_types.category for each flag in session_research_flags; (4) wa_meaning_sense rows per term; (5) mti_term_cross_refs per term; (6) wa_session_b_dimensions rows; (7) wa_dim_review_cluster_log for the registry's cluster; (8) word_run_state rows for this registry.
tasks.impact: Any section not yet in the export script requires a CC update before Session B v5.0 can run against the full spec

tasks.question: Are correlation signals embedded in the word export JSON, or provided as a separate file (e.g. wa-correlations-{date}.json)? If separate: what is the current filename and where does it live? What fields does it contain per signal?
tasks.impact: Pass 6 depends on correlation data. Session B needs to know whether to attach it separately or whether it comes through the export.

tasks.question: The statistics section includes god_as_subject and somatic_link consistency counts (comparing wa_term_inventory fields against mti_term_flags). Confirm that word_export.py can produce these derived counts, or whether they require a separate query at session start.
tasks.impact: Stage 1 audit checklist — consistency check between superseded fields and replacement fields

tasks.question: Produce a complete export for registry 023 (compassion) against this spec. Return the export file for Claude AI cross-reference against the Stage 1 audit checklist. Note in the export which sections are currently missing from word_export.py.
tasks.impact: Primary verification step — the live export will confirm what is present, what is absent, and what requires export script updates before Session B v5.0 runs
